observe: This world wasn't always shaped like this. Every rule you're learning was a scar first.
fault: You assumed it always managed things this way. It didn't — something broke, and this is the fix.
repair: Walk the timeline. Each era exists because the one before it failed somewhere.
escape: No time for the whole history. Name the era that matters right now.

observe: Operating systems evolved through distinct eras: batch, multiprogramming, time-sharing, modern.
fault: An assumption about OS functionality doesn't match its actual historical origin.
repair: Review each era and the problem it was designed to solve.
escape: Identify the correct era before the timeline closes.

hints: Each era's OS did less than the one after it — work backward from what's missing.
